title: Notch Release Button
prompt:
Create a production-ready Notch Release Button using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original control represents releasing a reviewed change: a coral notch slides from the left edge into a small cutout on the right after activation, while the label changes between Hold release and Release ready. Put it on a light Bento surface with white and #F4F5F6 backgrounds, a #ECECEE border, 22px corners, a soft neutral shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F supporting copy. Coral is the only bright accent and neither the card nor button may use a gradient.

Use one native button with a 44px minimum target, aria-pressed, an explicit accessible name, and a polite status line. Click, Enter, and Space must reverse the state safely. Move the notch with a spring using stiffness 360 and damping 28; use a 160ms ease-out label fade and a 90ms press scale of 0.98. Keep the result deterministic with local state only: no timers, random values, storage, remote assets, or network calls. Use transform and opacity instead of layout work.

The compact presentation must remain legible at 320px and expand naturally in a large preview without fixed positioning. Include a visible 3px focus ring, logical tab order, AA contrast, and both text and shape cues. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the notch and retaining only a short opacity change. Return full paste-ready client code plus a runnable pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [ready,setReady]=React.useState(false);return
REVIEW GATE

{ready?'Release path is open.':'Release remains held for review.'}

}return ;})()
